result:
Build is green and no temporary instrumentation was added.

**Root cause:** `lua_lex::new_string` (crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:847) only routed through `intern_str_stub` → `LuaState::intern_str`, which dedupes short strings via the global pool but creates a fresh `GcRef<LuaString>` for every long-string call (crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1192). C-Lua's `luaX_newstring` uses a per-chunk anchor table (`ls->h`) to give the same TString to every occurrence of the same literal — including long ones (>40 bytes). That dedup was stubbed out, so `local s1 <const> = "...50 chars..."` and `local s2 <const> = "...50 chars..."` ended up as two different objects, breaking `string.format("%p", s1) == string.format("%p", s2)` at line 225 of literals.lua.

**Fix:** Added a `long_str_anchor: HashMap<Vec<u8>, GcRef<LuaString>>` field on `LexState` (crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:417) and made `new_string` consult/populate it before falling through to `intern_str_stub`. Wired the field's initializer into the one `lua_lex::LexState { ... }` construction in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:4423`.

**New state:** literals.lua no longer fails with `assertion failed!`. It now progresses to a different, unrelated failure: `coroutine.yield: coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)` — the string-identity bug is fully resolved.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/test_concat.lua << 'EOF'
do
  local function getadd (s) return string.format("%p", s) end
  local s1 <const> = "01234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789"
  local a1 = getadd(s1)
  print("a1=", a1)
  local sd = "0123456789" .. "0123456789012345678901234567890123456789"
  print("sd=", sd)
  print("sd==s1?", sd == s1)
  print("getadd(sd)=", getadd(sd))
  print("getadd(sd)~=a1?", getadd(sd) ~= a1)
  print("#sd=", #sd, "#s1=", #s1)
end
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/test_concat.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -20
touch crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs && cargo build -p lua-cli 2>&1 | tail -5
cargo clean -p lua-lex -p lua-parse 2>&1 | tail -3 ; cargo build -p lua-cli 2>&1 | tail -10
